source_pr.title: correct "order by", "having", "group by"s' resolving rules.
source_pr.body:
support order by item retrieve from "FROM CLAUSE". 
e.g. select id as c1 from src order by c1 + src.name.

symptoms: r = tk.MustQuery("select id as c1, name from select_order_test order by 2, id limit 1 offset 0;") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: r = tk.MustQuery("select id as c1, name from select_order_test order by 2, id limit 1 offset 0;") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: r = tk.MustQuery("select id as c1, name from select_order_test order by 2, id limit 1 offset 0;") (executor/executor_test.go)

files: ast/dml.go, executor/builder.go, executor/executor.go, executor/executor_test.go, executor/new_builder.go, executor/new_executor.go, expression/expression.go, expression/expression_test.go, plan/column_pruning.go, plan/expression_rewriter.go, plan/new_plan_test.go, plan/new_plans.go, plan/newplanbuilder.go, plan/optimizer.go, plan/planbuilder.go, plan/predicate_push_down.go, plan/stringer.go
